author: Macmill-340
text:
Welcome to PyFyve.

Most coding tutorials follow the same pattern: you get stuck, you ask for help, and the answer is handed to you — either by a person, an automated solution, or an AI. You copy it, it runs, and you move on. Three lessons later you're stuck again, because you never actually built the understanding. You borrowed it.

PyFyve works differently. When your code breaks, a small AI model called Fyve — running entirely on your computer — looks at your mistake and responds with a hint. Not the fix. It tells you what kind of error you made, reminds you of the rule you forgot, and asks a question that points you toward the answer. The last step is yours. You have to think, try again, and when it finally works, you'll know exactly why.

The experience is designed to be friction-free after the first setup. Everything runs locally — no internet connection needed at runtime, no account, no API key, and your code never leaves your machine. Your progress saves automatically, so you can close the app and pick up exactly where you left off.

Each lesson follows the same structure: a short explanation of a concept, a clear task, and a validator that checks whether your code meets the requirement. If it doesn't, Fyve steps in. If it does, you move on.

This is version 1. The curriculum covers the fundamentals — variables, printing, data types, strings, conditionals, lists, and loops. These are the building blocks that everything else in Python is made from. Future versions will extend the curriculum and deepen the challenge.

Press Enter when you're ready to begin.
